preamble: Insertion of the voiceless dental fricative [θ] from zero is extremely rare. The most plausible contexts involve hypercorrection by speakers learning or over-applying dental fricatives. Note: many cited examples (e.g., 'leŋkθ', 'warmpθ') actually show stop insertion BEFORE an existing [θ], which belongs to the empty_to_k / empty_to_p files, not here.

examples.note: Some speakers hypercorrect non-standard th-stopping (ð/θ→d/t) in reverse, inserting [θ] where [s/t] was expected. Flagged: genuine ∅→θ data is scarce.

sources: Ladefoged, P. & Johnson, K. (2015). A Course in Phonetics (7th ed.). Cengage Learning.
